heroes.note: MSCI downgrade risk (Jun 19 review), rupiah at record lows, capital outflows exceeding Rp67T YTD, policy intervention fears on commodities.
Index headed for seventh weekly decline; MSCI decision on Jun 19 critical; potential for further foreign selling and rupiah pressure persist.

news.title: JCI plunges to lowest since May 2021 on MSCI, rupiah fears
news.impact: Selling breadth accelerating; 623 losers vs 106 gainers signals capitulation, weakening technical support.

news.title: Parliament passes commodity export law via PT DSI effective June 1
news.impact: New export rules reshape commodity flows and FX supply channels; potential friction with market stabilization efforts.

news.title: Foreign outflows hit Rp67T YTD as FTSE removes 8 Indonesian stocks
news.impact: FTSE rebalancing on Jun 22 removes 8 stocks for low free-float; signals broader index exclusion risks ahead of MSCI review.

news.title: Fitch, Moody's negative outlook erodes sovereign debt appeal
news.impact: Credit concerns over fiscal discipline and governance weigh on local-currency bonds and near-term institutional capital flows.

news.title: Rupiah hits record low 18,020 as dollar index hits 2-month peak
news.impact: Currency weakness accelerates; BI intervention reserves down to 146.2B USD; imported inflation risk rises into H2 2026.

news.title: May inflation rises to 3.08%, above BI's 2.5% midpoint target
news.impact: Accelerating price pressures limit BI's room for rate cuts; may force hold or tightening if rupiah weakness persists.

regulations.title: Commodity Export via PT DSI Law
regulations.sub: All commodity exporters must report through DSI · Effective Jun 1, 2026

regulations.title: FTSE Rebalancing Removals
regulations.sub: 8 Indonesian stocks removed for low free-float · Effective Jun 22, 2026

regulations.title: MSCI Emerging Market Review
regulations.sub: Potential downgrade to Frontier status · Decision date Jun 19, 2026

precedents.html: In May 2013, JCI fell 13% in a month on BI rate hike signals; rupiah weak at 9,700/USD; recovery took 18 months post-Fed taper tantrum clarity.
During 2014–2015 commodity crash, IDX lost 27% and rupiah sank to 13,500/USD; structural reform and rate cuts stabilized market by Q3 2015.
Post-COVID March 2020 panic, IDX crashed 34% before BI cuts, FX intervention, and global liquidity revival sparked 42% rebound within 6 months.

geo.html: Middle East tensions → Oil prices spike → Indonesia trade surplus narrows → RBI intervention pressure mounts. Now: US–Iran standoff unresolved, crude >$95/bbl, April trade surplus collapsed to $0.09B.
China growth slowdown → Commodity demand falls → Coal, nickel prices weaken → Miner earnings drop, FX inflows dry. Now: Global PMI softening; ADRO, INCO margin compression expected Q2–Q3 2026.

US Fed tightening bias → Dollar strength → EM outflows → Rupiah depreciation, foreign equity sell-off. Now: Dollar index at 2-month highs; cumulative foreign outflow Rp67T ytd; MSCI downgrade threat on Jun 19.
